client.name: The SD Group
client.positioning: New premium personal brand for a Bergen County luxury agent

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Full-bleed autoplay video hero with stacked display type and dual CTAs
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Fixed top nav, logo-left, utility nav right; collapses to icon menu on mobile; transparent over hero, subtle solid on scroll
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: Dual CTAs in hero: 'Contact Ryan' (primary) + 'I'm New to Ryan' (secondary); Netflix 'Watch Now' as entertainment hook
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Editorial portrait-led; Ryan in tailored suits + NYC skyline; cinematic Netflix stills; aspirational lifestyle
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Interest-segmented newsletter (news / sales tips / personal vision) plus persistent Contact CTA
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Personality-forward brand: the agent IS the product. Heavy editorial cadence, media-empire feel.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Full-viewport autoplay slider (5s) over luxury property imagery with search bar overlay
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Burger-only nav (BUY/SELL/RENT/AGENTS/REGIONS/SERVICES); bold red wordmark; minimal chrome
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'Explore Listings' under hero search; mid-page 'Let's Connect' multi-field form with agent dropdown
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Architectural grandeur: Malibu, Marbella, Cancun, European châteaux — wide-lens, golden-hour
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: 'Let's Connect' form captures name/email/phone/preferred-time/address/agent-select; routes to agent dashboard
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Red-on-black is a signature — brand equity through one hero color. Magazine/editorial framing elevates beyond MLS.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Editorial full-bleed photography hero with search pill; rotating featured estates
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Thin fixed top bar with centered wordmark, utility links flanking; stays solid white
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: Primary 'Search' inline in hero; secondary 'Contact an Agent' contextual throughout
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Museum-grade architectural + landscape photography; desaturated, editorial, highly curated
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Agent-match inquiry forms per-listing; newsletter opt-in in footer
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Navy + gold = unimpeachable luxury. Quiet typography, editorial restraint — they never shout.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Large search-led hero 'Where do you want to go?' over rotating lifestyle stills
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Sticky top nav: Buy/Rent/Sell/Agents + secondary New Development / World of Elliman; solid white
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'Start Your Search' primary; 'View Our Exclusives' secondary hook into curated inventory
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Lifestyle + architectural; aspirational but approachable; US coastal palettes
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Search-first funnel → property detail → agent inquiry form; editorial newsletter
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Heritage luxury playbook — navy, gold, search-first. Safe but effective.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Full-width hero image with dark top gradient + search bar; text overlay
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Fixed transparent-to-solid 48px nav; hamburger drawer (286px) on mobile with accordions
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'See What I Qualify For' (mortgage prequal via Rocket); search-first primary action
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Residential aspirational with vertical composition for overlay
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Mortgage calculator → prequal capture; agent search; city-page retargeting
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Tech-company feel applied to real estate. Blue accent = differentiator in a sea of navy/gold.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Immersive content-opening slider with video backgrounds + layered imagery
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Centered-logo transparent nav, backdrop-blur on scroll; uppercase links with 2px tracking; side-drawer on mobile
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'Contact Us' with arrow icon, backdrop-blur + gold hover; persistent mobile contact widget
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Grayscale-filtered luxury with selective color; dramatic black-to-transparent gradients; 16:10 ratios
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Modal contact form over dark overlay; bottom-border-only inputs (minimalist)
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Gold-standard for a SOLO luxury agent. Didot + Gilroy + charcoal/gold is the exact reference for SD Group.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Full-screen video hero with property search bar + dark overlay
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Fixed serif-logo header; bordered 'Contact Us' button; smooth padding transition on scroll
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'Contact Us' outline button repeated throughout; newsletter in footer
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Beverly Hills / LA estates in 50-50 splits; warm, editorial, luxury architectural
sitesAnalyzed.leadFlow: Newsletter email + arrow; contact modal with disclaimers
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Cormorant + taupe/cream = the 'serene luxury' palette. Directly applicable to a female-led NJ luxury brand.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Full-screen fade-transition carousel of named estate listings
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Horizontal top bar with broad dropdown for global markets + service categories
sitesAnalyzed.ctaStrategy: 'Explore' buttons to listings/developments/careers; Register/Sign In and Search
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: High-res lifestyle interiors + waterfront; professional architectural
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Broad global brand feel. Teal accent is interesting but typography is too generic for a personal brand reference.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: KW-templated hero with local-market photography and property search
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Standard KW Command-driven top nav with Buy/Sell/Search/Agents/About; sticky on scroll

typography.display: Gotham / Proxima Nova style sans (KW brand)

sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Generic stock + local MLS photography; inconsistent quality
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: Brokerage floor — SD Group needs to visually leapfrog the parent-office template. Use this only as the compliance baseline to beat.

sitesAnalyzed.heroStyle: Sotheby's-franchise hero with local Bergen/Hudson estate photography + search
sitesAnalyzed.navStyle: Sotheby's global template — thin white sticky nav, wordmark centered
sitesAnalyzed.photographyStyle: Franchise-consistent editorial architectural; strong in Alpine/Saddle River estates
sitesAnalyzed.takeaway: The direct NJ luxury competitor. SD Group must differentiate with personality — Sotheby's template is strong but agent-anonymous.

designRecommendations.rationale: Sarah De Jesus is a new brand entering a Bergen County market dominated by Sotheby's franchises (Prominent Properties, Christie's) and corporate KW templates. 'Ethereal Glass' reads too tech/proptech for a relationship-based residential agent; 'Soft Structuralism' is too austere for a warm, referral-driven female-led brand. 'Editorial Luxury' — think Aaron Kirman + Josh Flagg + a hint of Sotheby's restraint — positions Sarah as the curated, magazine-grade alternative to both the template brokerages and the loud Compass/Serhant tech aesthetic. It signals: quiet confidence, editorial craft, Ridgewood-appropriate taste, and a personal brand worth a $2M+ listing.

paletteDirection.name: Champagne
paletteDirection.role: Accent — rules, small CTA borders, hover underline

paletteDirection.name: Moss
paletteDirection.role: Secondary accent — Bergen County botanical tie-in, section dividers

typographyPairing.display: Fraunces (variable serif, slight optical warmth, supports both editorial display and body-ish subheads)
typographyPairing.body: Geist (modern neo-grotesque sans with excellent numeric tabular for listing data)
typographyPairing.rationale: Fraunces gives the Didot/Cormorant luxury register without being a 1:1 Josh Flagg clone; Geist is crisp enough to sit beside listing prices, sq ft, and neighborhood data without feeling dated. Safe fallback pair: Editorial New + Outfit.

designRecommendations.heroConcept: A slow, cinematic 4K curtain-reveal of a Ridgewood Tudor/Colonial at golden hour, with 'Sarah De Jesus' and a single editorial tagline ('Bergen County, Brought Home.') dissolving into view as the user scrolls — no search bar, no stock drone, just presence.
antiPatterns: Generic stock drone shots of suburban cul-de-sacs — use real Ridgewood / Ho-Ho-Kus / Glen Rock property photography only
Corporate blue + white (KW Command default, Zillow) — it flattens the brand into brokerage noise
Lorem Ipsum / AI-generated testimonials — every quote must name a real client, town, and transaction type
Auto-playing music or voiceovers on page load — violates the 'quiet luxury' register
Hero search bar as the primary visual element — this is a personal brand, not an MLS portal
Inter / generic Google sans for display — instant AI-slop tell
Purple/violet gradients, nested translucent cards, and neon accent palettes
Carousel-of-everything homepage (Nest Seekers trap) — one idea per section, well-executed
MLS-iframe listing grids with inconsistent photo quality — only show curated, photographer-shot inventory
Red CTA buttons (avoid KW parent-brand collision)
Overuse of italics / script fonts to signal 'feminine luxury' — let typography restraint carry the elegance
Stat counters that animate on scroll with no context ('47 homes sold!' with no timeframe or market)

referenceStack.avoid: kwvillage-square.com (parent template), compass.com (too tech), nestseekers.com (too broad)
